comments.body: Why do you have a single join table for both relations? You need one for each, id boss to post and boss to organization.
Hibernate doesn't support using join tables for ternary relationships. The way to model this is that the table must be an entity, so you have to map org_post_fill as an entity.
Should I in the OrgPostFill class join the two tables with "@JoinColumn + @OneToOne" and then in the Boss class join OrgPostFill with "@JoinColumn + @OneToOne"?

title: Set up a relationship between three tables from one composite table in Spring Hibernate
body:
Hello. In the diagram I showed the desired type of the Boss class. I tried to use @JoinTable to join two entities Organization and Post, but I encountered the error "table org_post_fill cannot be rejoined". Please help me connect these entities, I have no idea how to do this.

@ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
@JoinTable(
        name = "org_post_fill",
        joinColumns = {@JoinColumn(name = "id_boss")},
        inverseJoinColumns = {@JoinColumn(name = "id_organization")}
)
private Organization organization;
@ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)
@JoinTable(
        name = "org_post_fill",
        joinColumns = {@JoinColumn(name = "id_boss")},
        inverseJoinColumns = {@JoinColumn(name = "id_post")}
)
private Post post;
